title: Enable Turnstile-protected public comments
goal: Re-enable anonymous public comment submission after the frontend can provide a valid Cloudflare Turnstile token.
scope: Add a public Turnstile site key environment variable for the web app.
Render the Turnstile widget on blog post comment forms.
Submit the widget token as turnstileToken to the existing comments API.
Keep the honeypot, moderation queue, rate limit, and backend Turnstile verification in place.
Update deployment env generation and validation for the public site key.
Add E2E coverage for successful pending comment submission and failure without a token.
verification: A real Turnstile token is required before public comment submission succeeds.
Submitted comments enter the pending moderation queue.
Approved comments render below the blog post.
The public form does not expose secrets in the client bundle.
Production release docs include the Turnstile site key and secret setup steps.
notes: Implemented public comment submission with Cloudflare Turnstile token handling.
Configured staging test keys and production Turnstile environment sync in the deploy path.
Verified successful comment submission on staging and production after restarting the API service with updated runtime env.
Added frontend and backend coverage for pending comment submission and moderation-sensitive paths.
